commit.message:
Check trains only within 35-min pre-departure to 90-min post-departure window

Instead of checking all morning trains from 6-10 AM and all evening trains
from 2-8 PM regardless of departure time, each train is now only checked
within its own active window:
- Start: 35 min before scheduled departure (catches 25-35 min on-time alert)
- Stop: 90 min after scheduled departure (handles delays up to 90 min)
- Fallback: if GTFS has no departure time, always check (safe default)

Reduces API calls from ~22 checks/train/day to ~62 checks/train/day
(within the window), but eliminates all checks outside the window which
were the majority. Per-train: ~120 checks → ~22 active checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(#105): add server status offline banner and detail sheet

Add ServerStatusService that monitors Firebase connectivity via periodic
server probes and NWPathMonitor for network reachability. When offline,
an orange "Offline" pill appears at the top of all tabs. Tapping it opens
a half-sheet (expandable to full) showing server status details, a 30-second
recheck countdown timer, what still works, and what's temporarily limited.

New files:
- ServerStatusService.swift — core monitoring with countdown timer
- OfflineBannerView.swift — tappable orange capsule badge
- ServerStatusSheet.swift — detail sheet with presentationDetents
- ServerStatusRow.swift — reusable status row component

fix(getCurrentUser): auto-create missing profile row before returning null

When public.users row is missing for an authenticated user (e.g. logged in before
ensureProfile was added), getCurrentUser() was returning null, causing every page
to redirect to /onboarding. Now calls ensureProfile (idempotent) and retries the
query instead of immediately returning null.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

research: prove maxPrimeGap_le, fix compilation (erdos-1138-oq-03) (#3380)

* research: constructive 2D Borsuk-Ulam via Tucker lemma (borsuk-ulam-oq-03-oq-03)

Build bridge from Tucker's lemma to 2D Borsuk-Ulam theorem using
dominant component labeling. Define antisymmetric difference g(x) =
f(x) - f(-x), prove oddness and continuity, formalize triangulated
disk structure and grid triangulation. State approximate and exact
BU theorems. 7 proved, 5 sorries, 2 axioms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove Dirichlet kernel lemmas and continuity consequence (fourier-series-oq-03)

Resolve 3 of 4 sorries in FourierSeriesOQ03.lean:
- dirichletKernel_at_zero: D_N(0) = 2N+1 via fourier_at_zero helper
- dirichletKernel_neg: conjugate symmetry via fourier_apply_neg/fourier_eval_neg
- dirichlet_at_continuity_point: Fourier converges to f(x₀) at continuity points

New helper lemmas: fourier_at_zero, fourier_apply_neg, fourier_eval_neg,
rightLimit_eq_of_continuousAt, leftLimit_eq_of_continuousAt

Remaining sorry: dirichlet_pointwise_convergence (main theorem, needs ~300 lines
of Riemann-Lebesgue + integral splitting infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: triangle removal process gap analysis (erdos-1155-oq-01)

- Prove complete_has_triangles' for variable Fin n
- Prove bfl_sandwich combining BFL upper+lower bounds
- Prove bfl_exponent_is_three_halves (exact critical exponent)
- Prove conjecture_iff_both_bounds decomposition
- Prove f_small_values_bound from complete graph edge count
- State sufficient conditions: limit convergence → full conjecture
- 10 sorries remaining (filter real analysis, finset extraction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: categorical structure of distributions under convolution (clt-oq-03)

- Formalize commutative monoid (ProbMeasure, convolution, Dirac delta)
- Prove convPow_add distribution law by induction
- State CLT as convergence in domain of attraction
- Formalize Gaussian stability and Cramér's indecomposability
- 1 sorry remaining (charFun base case)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: minimum edges for graph dimension d (erdos-1007-oq-01)

Formalize the minEdges(d) function and prove structural results:
- Small-dimension completeness (d≤3): K_{d+1} is optimal
- d=4 surprise: K_{3,3} beats K₅ (9 < 10 edges)
- d=5 returns to complete graph pattern
- Linear lower bound and quadratic upper bound
- Open conjectures: monotonicity, Θ(d²) growth, K_{d+1} optimality

1 sorry (Nat→ℝ cast with division in upper_bound_quadratic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove maxPrimeGap_le, fix compilation errors (erdos-1138-oq-03)

- Proved maxPrimeGap_le: every prime gap q-p ≤ q ≤ x, so sSup ≤ x
  via csSup_le (nonempty) and csSup_empty + bot_le (empty case)
- Removed stale import Mathlib.Order.Filter.AtTopBot (no longer exists)
- Fixed primeCounting_zero proof (simp already closes, decide redundant)
- Fixed primeCounting_le proof (explicit calc for Finset.card_filter_le)
- File now compiles with 0 sorries (axioms for open conjectures intentional)

commit.message:
refactor: reduce boilerplate in discovery module spec builders with macro (#773) (#783)

* refactor: reduce boilerplate in discovery module spec builders with macro (#773)

Introduce `discovery_spec!` macro that encapsulates the repetitive
clone → guard → detect → convert → wrap pipeline shared by all 43
discovery module specs. Four macro variants handle the different
patterns: standard guard, post-record guard, minimum-count guard,
and custom closure.

The four spec files (neuron, synapse, structural, scoring) are reduced
by 54% (1317 → 607 lines) while preserving identical behaviour.

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-06-S11b: c_0 concentration theorem — only c_0 varies for regular tournaments

For regular tournaments at n=7: c_2=9I, c_4=180I, c_6=720I are ALL universal.
Only c_0 varies between H-classes. At n=5: c_2 eigenvalues determined by score
sequence only. Hierarchy: score->c_2, c_0 eigenvalues->H, c_0 eigenvectors->iso class.

commit.message:
Fix Dogecoin-specific runtime errors in dunes and inscription indexing

- Rename rune_validation.rs -> dune_validation.rs (Dogecoin naming)
- Stub rune_etching_has_valid_commit: Dogecoin Dunes has no Taproot
  commit-reveal scheme (no SegWit/witness data); accept all etchings
  until Dune-native validation rules are confirmed
- Disable jubilee block height: return u64::MAX so sequence_cursor.reset()
  never fires on Dogecoin (no Ordinals jubilee event exists on Doge)
- Mark parse_inscriptions_from_witness as dead code; Dogecoin inscription
  parsing uses script_sig + from_transactions_dogecoin exclusively

commit.message:
feat: complete DOMAIN type catalog and engine integration

Implements CREATE DOMAIN and DROP DOMAIN with full catalog persistence
and engine execution. This completes the infrastructure started in commit
b5d5997, adding the missing catalog functions and engine integration.

Catalog changes (src/sql/catalog.zig):
- Add makeDomainKey() for domain: prefix namespace (separate from enum types)
- Add createDomain() for storing domain definitions with base type and optional constraint
- Add getDomain() for retrieving domain metadata
- Add dropDomain() for removing domain definitions
- Add domainExists() for checking domain existence
- Serialization: [base_type: u8][has_constraint: u8][constraint_len: u16][constraint_text]
- Add 10 comprehensive tests covering basic operations, conflicts, corruption handling

Engine changes (src/sql/engine.zig):
- Add CREATE DOMAIN statement execution with catalog persistence
- Add DROP DOMAIN statement execution with IF EXISTS support
- Constraint expressions stored in AST but not yet enforced (TODO: serialize to text)
- Add 8 integration tests covering basic operations, conflicts, IF EXISTS

Test coverage:
- catalog.zig: 10 new tests (basic, no-constraint, duplicate, table/enum conflicts, drop, exists, corrupted data)
- engine.zig: 8 new tests (basic, no-constraint, drop, IF EXISTS, conflicts)
- Total: 18 new tests, all passing

Note: Constraint enforcement not yet implemented - constraints are parsed and
stored but not validated. This will be added in a future milestone when CHECK
constraint infrastructure is built.

commit.message:
Support FIFO listen addresses for socket discovery and writing

The socket writer previously only handled Stream (Unix socket) types,
but systemd services using StandardInput=socket typically use
ListenFIFO. Discovery now prefers Stream > FIFO > first available,
and the writer dispatches between UnixStream and file I/O accordingly.
Manual --socket paths auto-detect FIFO vs socket via stat.

filtering from the receiver backend (~550 lines)

from the receiver UI (~437 lines)

connect/disconnect

- [x] All 64 receiver-ui Vitest tests pass

`start_paused` Tokio attr)

immediately without needing to resume

Persist dashboard log and stats across server restarts with session/total split

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ad monetization framework and Play Store cross-promo

Ad integration (config-driven, zero impact until publisher ID set):
- ads.js: AdSense loader, banner placement, interstitial between games
- Interstitial shown every 2nd game over with 60s cooldown
- 5-second countdown before close button activates
- Graceful degradation: no publisher ID = no ads loaded
- Ad blocker resilient (catches load failures silently)

Play Store cross-promo banner (works immediately, no AdSense needed):
- Links to com.dohrbusiness.blockpuzzle on Google Play
- Styled as subtle gradient banner with Install CTA
- Hidden on very short screens (<580px height)

Game flow updated:
- Game over sequence: interstitial ad -> dismiss -> game over modal
- Safety timeout prevents ad from blocking game over forever

To activate AdSense:
1. Apply at adsense.google.com
2. Set publisherId in ads.js
3. Create banner + interstitial ad units, set slot IDs
4. Deploy - ads go live immediately
